overview: Define a reusable base container image for all loops that standardizes runtime behavior, includes Codex CLI, ships common developer tooling, bundles internal binaries, and supports mounting skills as a volume at a fixed in-container path.
goals: Provide one consistent base image definition used by every loop in the system
Include Codex CLI and a practical developer toolchain out of the box
Bundle internal binaries in the runtime image via deterministic multi-stage build
Guarantee a stable skills mount location at /home/dev/.codex/skills
nonGoals: No orchestration or runtime deployment-definition changes outside image definition
No CI/CD pipeline wiring changes in this phase
successMetrics: All target loops can build and start successfully using this base image without per-loop environment customization
Codex CLI, required developer tools, and internal binaries are available in runtime smoke tests
Security quality gate reports zero critical vulnerabilities at merge time
openQuestions: Which exact internal binary names and versions must be bundled in v1?
Which container registry/repository path should publish the canonical base image?
Should pnpm be installed via corepack or standalone package manager install?
What is the minimum supported CPU architecture set (amd64 only vs amd64+arm64)?

stack.hosting: Existing container registry in current infrastructure (TBD)
stack.auth: Not applicable for the image artifact; runtime auth remains owned by loop services

uiNotes: No UI routes or screens are required; deliverable is a container image definition and verification scripts.

importFormat.description: Not applicable. No external import payload is required for this feature.

rules: Base runtime image must be Alpine-based.
Codex CLI must be installed during image build and be executable in the final runtime image.
Developer toolchain must include: git, curl, jq, bash, ca-certificates, make, node, npm or pnpm, python3, pip, and ripgrep.
Internal binaries must be produced in a builder stage and copied into the runtime stage.
Skills mount path is fixed at /home/dev/.codex/skills and must be readable by the runtime user.
Image verification must include smoke test, lint checks, vulnerability scan, and SBOM generation.
No CI/CD pipeline integration changes are part of this iteration; commands must still run locally/reproducibly.
qualityGates: docker run --rm loop-base:local sh -lc 'codex --version && git --version && node --version && python3 --version && rg --version'

stories.title: Define Alpine-based base image skeleton and runtime user
stories.description: As a platform engineer, I want a canonical Alpine-based Dockerfile skeleton so every loop starts from a consistent runtime baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update Dockerfile to use an Alpine base image and define a non-root runtime user with a stable home directory.
Set default working directory and shell behavior required by loop execution.
Example: running `docker run --rm loop-base:local sh -lc 'id && pwd'` shows non-root user and expected working directory.
Negative case: if Dockerfile uses non-Alpine base, validation fails with explicit message and build is rejected.
Document the base image tag and rationale in repo docs.

stories.title: Install Codex CLI in runtime image
stories.description: As a loop runtime consumer, I want Codex CLI preinstalled so loop agents can run without manual bootstrapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Codex CLI installation steps to Docker build and ensure binary is available on PATH in final stage.
Use latest install channel during build (no version pin), as specified for v1.
Example: `docker run --rm loop-base:local codex --version` returns a valid semantic version string.
Negative case: if install step fails or binary is missing from PATH, smoke test exits non-zero and story is not complete.
Record the installation command in Dockerfile comments or docs for maintainability.

stories.title: Add common developer tooling to image
stories.description: As an engineer running loops, I want common development tools preinstalled so scripts and diagnostics work consistently across loops.
acceptanceCriteria: Install required packages in Dockerfile with explicit commands, including `apk add --no-cache bash git curl jq ca-certificates make python3 py3-pip ripgrep nodejs npm` (or Alpine-equivalent package names available in target repo).
Install pnpm support via approved mechanism (corepack enable or explicit package install) and document which method was used.
Example: running tool checks in container returns success for git, curl, jq, make, node, npm/pnpm, python3, pip, and rg.
Negative case: if any required tool command is missing, smoke test reports the exact missing binary and fails.
Keep final image layer changes minimal by cleaning package caches where applicable.

stories.title: Bundle internal binaries via multi-stage build
stories.description: As a platform engineer, I want internal binaries compiled in a builder stage and copied into runtime so builds are repeatable and runtime remains slim.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement multi-stage Docker build where internal binaries are produced in a dedicated builder stage and copied into the runtime stage.
Place binaries in a documented runtime directory on PATH (for example `/usr/local/bin`).
Example: runtime container executes each required internal binary with `--version` successfully.
Negative case: if a binary is present in builder but not copied to runtime, smoke verification fails and names the missing binary.
Document builder inputs and expected artifacts so future binary additions follow the same pattern.

stories.title: Support skills volume mount contract
stories.description: As a loop executor, I want a stable in-container skills mount path so external skill volumes can be attached consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create and document fixed skills directory `/home/dev/.codex/skills` with runtime-user read access.
Ensure runtime behavior does not overwrite mounted content at startup.
Example: with `-v $(pwd)/tmp-skills:/home/dev/.codex/skills`, container lists mounted files from host.
Negative case: starting container without mounted skills must not fail; path exists and remains empty/readable.
Add a mount-path smoke test script that validates both mounted and unmounted scenarios.

stories.title: Add reproducible verification and security gates
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want deterministic quality gate commands for build, smoke, lint, vulnerability scanning, and SBOM generation so image quality is enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide script or documented command set that runs all top-level `qualityGates` in order and exits non-zero on first failure.
Include lint tooling invocation commands (`hadolint Dockerfile` and `shellcheck scripts/*.sh`) and required setup notes.
Include security scan command `trivy image --severity CRITICAL --exit-code 1 loop-base:local` and SBOM command `syft packages loop-base:local -o spdx-json > artifacts/sbom-loop-base.spdx.json`.
Example: running the full gate sequence after a valid build completes successfully and writes the SBOM artifact file.
Negative case: introducing a known critical-vulnerability image tag causes trivy gate to fail with non-zero exit code.
No CI/CD workflow files are modified in this story; only local/reusable command definitions are added.

stories.title: Document base image usage in loop definitions
stories.description: As a loop author, I want clear usage documentation so each new loop can reference this base image and mount skills correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add documentation showing how loop definitions reference the base image and mount skills at `/home/dev/.codex/skills`.
Include examples for local development and production-like execution contexts.
Example: sample loop definition starts successfully using the base image and mounted skills path.
Negative case: misconfigured mount path example is documented with expected failure symptoms and correction.
Documentation explicitly lists all bundled tools and internal binaries verified by quality gates.
